Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the normative process behavior and the desiderata The normative rules are strict and must be followed while the desiderata are recommendations that are nice to have but not mandatory

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Starting with Part 1 Normative Process Behavior The strict sequence is Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Inform Applicant If Approved Finalize Documentation Close Case The key constraints are about the order of activities and specific conditions based on the loan decision

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the event log there are three cases 1 2 and 3 Lets check each case against the normative rules

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Activities in order Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case This seems to follow the sequence correctly Since the loan was approved they finalized documentation before closing No issues here So Case 1 is compliant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 The first activity is Application Received Then the next activity is Risk Assessment at 2024 01 11 08 20 But according to the rules Credit Check must come before Risk Assessment Here Risk Assessment is done before Credit Check which is a violation Then Credit Check is done after Risk Assessment which is out of order Then Loan Decision Rejected Finalize Documentation Close Case But the rules state that if the loan is rejected you should not finalize documentation Here they did finalize documentation after rejection which is another violation So Case 2 has two normative violations out of order activities and finalizing documentation after rejection

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 The sequence starts correctly with Application Received Then Credit Check is on 2024 01 14 which is two days later But the desiderata mention that Credit Check should be within 24 hours but thats a desideratum not a normative rule So normatively the order is correct Application Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case The order is correct However the timing between Application and Credit Check is a desideratum issue not a normative one Similarly the time between Loan Decision and Inform Applicant is longer than recommended 48 hours but again thats a desideratum Also theres no Follow up Call which is a desideratum So Case 3 doesnt violate any normative rules but fails on desiderata

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Now for Part 2 Desiderata These are the recommended best practices For each case check if they meet the timing recommendations and if a Follow up Call was made

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Credit Check was done 30 minutes after Application Received which is within 24 hours Good Inform Applicant was 2 hours after Loan Decision which is within 48 hours Good Follow up Call Not present in log So desideratum failure

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Credit Check timing Application was at 08 00 Credit Check at 09 00 1 hour later So within 24 hours Inform Applicant was at 10 00 which is 30 minutes after Loan Decision 09 30 So within 48 hours Follow up Call Not present So desiderata failure for missing Follow up Call

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Credit Check was on 2024 01 14 at 10 00 Application was on 2024 01 12 at 09 00 Thats more than 24 hours So desiderata violation for timing Loan Decision was at 12 00 on Jan 14 Inform Applicant at 15 00 on Jan 16 Thats more than 48 hours So another desiderata violation No Follow up Call explicitly noted So three desiderata violations

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait but in the event log for Case 3 theres a note No Follow up Call For Case 1 the log doesnt mention it either So both Case 1 and 3 might be missing the Follow up Call Similarly Case 2 also doesnt have it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Perhaps the other cases did have it but its not listed Or maybe the log is incomplete This is a bit ambiguous

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem says it is encouraged to perform a Follow up Call before closing the case So if the log doesnt show it then it wasnt done Therefore all three cases are missing the Follow up Call which is a desiderata failure

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So summarizing Normative violations Case 2 Out of order activities Risk Assessment before Credit Check and finalizing documentation after rejection Desiderata failures Case 1 Missing Follow up Call Case 2 Missing Follow up Call Case 3 Credit Check delayed Inform Applicant delayed and missing Follow up Call

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For Case 2s normative violations Maybe there was a misunderstanding of the process or an error in the system that allowed out of order steps For finalizing documentation after rejection perhaps a mistake was made where the staff assumed approval but it was rejected

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For desiderata in Case 3 The delay in Credit Check could be due to high workload or system delays The delay in informing the applicant might be due to holidays or weekends The missing Follow up Call could be due to oversight or time constraints
